title: ETS match spec arena allocator — eliminate Box::leak in production code
purpose: The ETS match spec evaluator uses a LeakingAllocator that calls Box::leak to produce match result tuples and lists. These allocations are never freed, causing unbounded memory growth in long-running processes that use ETS match operations. The no_box_leak CI gate test correctly catches this. BEAM solves this with per-table or per-operation allocators. This brief replaces LeakingAllocator with an arena-backed allocator that owns the memory and frees it when the match results are consumed.
task: Replace the LeakingAllocator in ets/match_spec.rs with an arena allocator that owns heap memory for match spec result terms. The arena should live as long as the match operation's result set. When results are copied into a process heap (the normal consumption path), the arena can be dropped. This fixes the no_box_leak CI gate test.

requirements.title: MatchArena owned allocator
requirements.spec: Create a MatchArena struct in crates/beamr/src/ets/match_arena.rs that implements TermAllocator. It shall own a Vec<Box<[u64]>> of allocation blocks. alloc_tuple and alloc_list allocate from new blocks pushed onto the Vec. The arena frees all memory when dropped.
acceptance: Dropping MatchArena frees all allocated memory
Terms produced by MatchArena are valid as long as the arena is alive

requirements.title: Replace LeakingAllocator with MatchArena
requirements.spec: Replace all uses of LeakingAllocator in match_spec.rs with MatchArena. The CompiledMatchSpec::run method shall accept or create a MatchArena and return results tied to its lifetime. Remove the LeakingAllocator struct entirely.
acceptance: No Box::leak calls remain in match_spec.rs
All existing ETS match spec tests pass
The no_box_leak CI gate test passes

requirements.title: Result copying to process heap
requirements.spec: ETS select/match operations that return results to a process shall deep-copy arena-allocated terms into the process heap before the arena is dropped. Add a copy_term_to_process utility if one does not already exist.
acceptance: ETS select returns terms allocated on the process heap, not the arena
Arena is dropped after results are copied
Process heap terms remain valid after arena drop

boundaries: Arena is per-match-operation, not per-table — keeps memory bounded to operation scope
No changes to the ETS table data storage model (entries still live in table-owned memory)
OUT OF SCOPE: Full ETS-owned heap (BEAM's per-table allocator) — arena per match operation is sufficient
OUT OF SCOPE: Concurrent arena access — match operations are single-threaded per operation
verification: grep -r 'Box::leak' crates/beamr/src/ returns only src/atom/table.rs hits
No LeakingAllocator remains in the codebase
